description: Wave Statistics & Predictions - Analyze wastewater Covid waves and forecast next values using ARIMA

userStories.title: Remove Rougeole chart from dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I no longer need the Rougeole chart cluttering the dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: RougeoleChart component removed from page.tsx render and its import removed
Rougeole mention removed from page description text (keep Covid-19, bronchiolite, grippe)
Component file src/components/rougeole-chart.tsx can stay (no dead-code cleanup required)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wave detection algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a wave detection algorithm that identifies individual Covid waves from the smoothed wastewater time series.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/lib/wave-detection.ts exports a detectWaves(series) function
Input: array of { week: string, value: number | null } sorted by week ascending
Uses derivative-based approach on smoothed values: wave starts when sustained upward trend begins, ends when value returns to baseline
Each detected wave object includes: startWeek, peakWeek, endWeek, peakValue, duration (weeks), amplitude (peak minus baseline)
Handles edge cases: incomplete waves at series boundaries are excluded, null/missing values are skipped
Export the Wave type interface from the module
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use smoothed_value from the DB, not raw value. National station ID is 'national_54'.

userStories.title: Unit tests for wave detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests to verify wave detection works correctly on synthetic data.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file src/lib/__tests__/wave-detection.test.ts (or .spec.ts)
Test cases: single wave (triangle shape), multiple waves, flat data (no waves), data with null gaps, noisy data with small fluctuations that should not count as waves
Tests verify correct startWeek, peakWeek, endWeek, duration, amplitude for each detected wave
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use vitest or jest depending on what the project already uses. Check package.json for test runner.

userStories.title: Wave statistics computation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to compute summary statistics from detected waves.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/lib/wave-stats.ts exports a computeWaveStats(waves) function
Input: array of Wave objects from wave-detection.ts
Returns object with: waveCount, avgDuration, stdDuration, avgFrequency (waves/year), stdFrequency, avgAmplitude, stdAmplitude, avgInterWaveGap (weeks), stdInterWaveGap
All stats include mean + standard deviation
Edge cases: 0 waves returns all nulls, 1 wave returns count and amplitude but null for std/frequency/gap
Unit tests covering 0, 1, 2, and 5+ wave inputs
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use simple-statistics npm package if helpful, or compute mean/std manually (it's just a few lines).

userStories.title: Install arima package and create forecast module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an ARIMA-based forecast of the wastewater signal to predict the next 3 weeks.
acceptanceCriteria: npm install arima (add to dependencies)
New module src/lib/forecast.ts exports a forecastWastewater(series, horizonWeeks?) function
Input: array of { week: string, value: number | null } sorted by week ascending
Default horizonWeeks = 3
Uses arima npm package to fit ARIMA model on the smoothed values (filter out nulls)
Returns array of { week, predictedValue, lowerBound, upperBound } for each forecast week
Week strings are computed by incrementing ISO weeks from the last data point
Confidence interval (95%) widens over time
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The arima npm package API: new ARIMA({ p, d, q, s, verbose: false }).train(data).predict(steps). Returns [predictions, errors]. Use auto mode if available. ISO week incrementing: parse 'YYYY-WXX' format, add weeks, handle year boundaries.

userStories.title: Unit tests for forecast module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests to verify the forecast module produces reasonable output.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file for forecast.ts
Test: sinusoidal input produces 3-week forecast with values in reasonable range
Test: flat/constant input produces near-constant forecast
Test: forecast returns correct number of weeks (default 3)
Test: week strings are correctly incremented from last input week
Test: lowerBound <= predictedValue <= upperBound for each forecast point
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: tRPC endpoints for wave stats and forecast
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need API endpoints to fetch wave statistics and forecast data.
acceptanceCriteria: New tRPC router src/server/trpc/routers/wave-analysis.ts

Procedure waveAnalysis.getWaveStats: input { stationId?: string } (default 'national_54'), queries wastewater_indicators for that station ordered by week, runs detectWaves + computeWaveStats, returns { waves: Wave[], stats: WaveStats }

Procedure waveAnalysis.getForecast: input { stationId?: string } (default 'national_54'), queries wastewater_indicators smoothed_value for that station, runs forecastWastewater, returns forecast array
Both procedures query DB directly (reuse existing wastewater_indicators table)
Router registered in root router at src/server/trpc/router.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing pattern in wastewater.ts router. Use smoothed_value column for both wave detection and forecasting.

userStories.title: Forecast snapshots DB table and sync integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want national forecast predictions persisted so I can later compare predictions vs reality.
acceptanceCriteria: New table forecast_snapshots in src/server/db/schema.ts: id (serial PK), created_at (timestamp, defaultNow), target_week (varchar), predicted_value (doublePrecision), lower_bound (doublePrecision), upper_bound (doublePrecision)
Unique constraint on (created_at cast to date, target_week) — or use a snapshot_date (date) column with unique on (snapshot_date, target_week) for simplicity
Generate Drizzle migration with db:generate

In /api/sync route (src/app/api/sync/route.ts): after wastewater sync completes, fetch national_54 smoothed data, run forecastWastewater, insert 3 rows into forecast_snapshots (one per predicted week), use onConflictDoNothing to skip if already run today
New tRPC procedure waveAnalysis.getForecastHistory: returns all past snapshots ordered by created_at desc, target_week asc
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only national_54 forecasts are stored — keep it lightweight. Follow existing sync pattern in src/app/api/sync/route.ts.

userStories.title: Render forecast on main chart as grey curve with confidence band
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the predicted wastewater trend as a grey line continuing from the last real data point, with a shaded confidence band.
acceptanceCriteria: In WastewaterChart component, call trpc.waveAnalysis.getForecast with the selected station ID
Forecast data merged into chart data array — forecast rows have predictedValue, lowerBound, upperBound fields while real data rows have null for these
Forecast line rendered as grey dashed Line on the existing ComposedChart (color: hsl(0, 0%, 60%), strokeDasharray: '5 5')
Confidence band rendered as grey semi-transparent Area between lowerBound and upperBound (fillOpacity ~0.15)
Forecast starts exactly where real data ends — last real data point value bridges to first forecast point
X-axis extends to accommodate forecast weeks
Legend entry 'Prévision' with grey styling, toggleable via existing hiddenKeys mechanism
Loading state while forecast computes (do not block main chart render)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use Recharts Area component for the confidence band. The ComposedChart already supports mixing Line and Area. Forecast query can run in parallel with main data query.

userStories.title: Wave statistics panel on dashboard
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see key wave statistics in a section on the dashboard that replaces the Rougeole chart.
acceptanceCriteria: New component src/components/wave-stats-panel.tsx
Calls trpc.waveAnalysis.getWaveStats with selected station ID (from useStationPreferences — use first selected station or 'national_54' if national is selected)
Card-based layout showing: wave count, avg duration ± std (weeks), avg frequency ± std (waves/year), avg amplitude ± std, avg gap between waves ± std (weeks)
If forecast data available: show 'Prochaine vague estimée: semaine YYYY-WXX' with confidence range
Values formatted in French locale (comma for decimals)
Responsive: 2-col grid on mobile, 4-col on desktop (use Tailwind grid)
Loading skeleton while data fetches (reuse existing Skeleton component)
Added to page.tsx below the chart+map row (where Rougeole was)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Stats update when station selection or date range changes. Use shadcn Card component. Follow existing patterns for loading states.
